factors.summary: Management has a positive forward guidance for 2019, expecting continued positive impact from backlog and acquisitions.

evidence.text: For 2019, the Company’s strong backlog, the full year impact of the 2018 acquisitions and continued focus on and implementation of Operational Excellence initiatives are expected to have a positive impact on the remainder of the Company’s 2019 results.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in EIG, which saw margins remain flat despite lower margins from recent acquisitions, and EMG, which saw margins improve.

evidence.text: EIG’s operating margins were 25.6% of net sales for the first six months of 2019, compared with 25.8% of net sales for the first six months of 2018. EIG’s operating margins for the first six months of 2019 declined primarily from the impact of lower margins on recent acquisitions, which had a 1.0% negative impact, partially offset by the benefits of the Group’s Operational Excellence initiatives. EMG’s operating margins were 21.0% of net sales for the first six months of 2019, compared with 20.1% of net sales for the first six months of 2018.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, with a 1.0% increase in EMG's net sales and a 3% increase in EIG's net sales, suggesting real demand rather than just price increases.

evidence.text: EMG’s net sales totaled $949.9 million for the first six months of 2019, an increase of $29.2 million or 3.2%, compared with $920.7 million for the first six months of 2018. The net sales increase was due to 5% organic sales growth and an unfavorable 2% effect of foreign currency translation. EIG’s net sales totaled $1,627.2 million for the first six months of 2019, an increase of $166.3 million or 11.4%, compared with $1,460.9 million for the first six months of 2018. The net sales increase was due to 4% organic sales growth, a 9% increase from the 2018 acquisitions of Motec, Spectro Scientific Corporation, Forza and Telular Corporation, partially offset by an unfavorable 1% effect of foreign currency translation.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Both EIG and EMG segments are performing well, with strong international sales in Europe and Asia, indicating momentum in these end markets.

evidence.text: Both reportable segments of the Company maintain strong international sales presences in Europe and Asia. EIG’s operating income was $416.0 million for the first six months of 2019, an increase of $38.8 million or 10.3%, compared with $377.2 million for the first six months of 2018. EMG’s operating income was $199.9 million for the first six months of 2019, an increase of $14.6 million or 7.9%, compared with $185.3 million for the first six months of 2018.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Input costs are increasing, particularly in cost of sales, which rose by 5.9% in the second quarter and 7.7% in the first six months, driven by higher net sales.

evidence.text: Cost of sales for the second quarter of 2019 was $838.2 million or 65.0% of net sales, an increase of $47.0 million or 5.9%, compared with $791.2 million or 65.4% of net sales for the second quarter of 2018. Cost of sales increased primarily due to the increase in net sales noted above. Cost of sales for the first six months of 2019 was $1,689.5 million or 65.6% of net sales, an increase of $121.5 million or 7.7%, compared with $1,568.0 million or 65.8% of net sales for the first six months of 2018.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings, contributing to improved margins and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: The increase in segment operating income and segment operating margins for the first six months of 2019 resulted primarily from the increase in net sales noted above, as well as the benefits of the Company’s Operational Excellence initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving due to the benefits of the Company’s Operational Excellence initiatives, which have positively impacted margins.

evidence.text: The increase in segment operating income and segment operating margins for the first six months of 2019 resulted primarily from the increase in net sales noted above, as well as the benefits of the Company’s Operational Excellence initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing slightly, with a 3.8% increase in interest expense driven by new senior notes, indicating cautious expansion.

evidence.text: Interest expense was $21.5 million for the second quarter of 2019, an increase of $0.7 million or 3.4%, compared with $20.8 million for the second quarter of 2018. The change in interest expense is largely driven by the 2018 private placement senior notes issued in December 2018 ($475 million and 75 million Euros) and January 2019 ($100 million), partially offset by a decrease related to the repayment in full, at maturity, of $80 million in aggregate principal amount of 6.35% private placement senior notes and $160 million in aggregate principal amount of 7.08% private placement senior notes in the third quarter of 2018, and $65 million in aggregate principal amount of 7.18% private placement senior notes in the fourth quarter of 2018.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving as net debt-to-capital ratio decreased from 34.9% to 28.9%, indicating deleveraging.

evidence.text: The debt-to-capital ratio was 34.6% at June 30, 2019, compared with 38.3% at December 31, 2018. The net debt-to-capital ratio (total debt, net less cash and cash equivalents divided by the sum of net debt and stockholders’ equity) was 28.9% at June 30, 2019, compared with 34.9% at December 31, 2018.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing with a $500 million authorization for common stock repurchase, indicating management confidence.

evidence.text: Effective February 12, 2019, the Company’s Board of Directors approved an increase of $500 million in the authorization for the repurchase of the Company’s common stock.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, with a 16.3% increase in cash provided by operating activities, supporting favorable working capital trends.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities totaled $442.6 million for the first six months of 2019, an increase of $62.1 million or 16.3%, compared with $380.5 million for the first six months of 2018. The increase in cash provided by operating activities for the first six months of 2019 was primarily due to higher net income.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is creating value through accretive growth, particularly with recent acquisitions contributing to net sales increases.

evidence.text: The net sales increase was due to 4% organic sales growth, a 9% increase from the 2018 acquisitions of Motec, Spectro Scientific Corporation, Forza and Telular Corporation, partially offset by an unfavorable 1% effect of foreign currency translation.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are having a negative impact, particularly an unfavorable 1% effect in the second quarter and 2% effect in the first six months, which have slightly offset revenue growth.

evidence.text: The increase in net sales for the second quarter of 2019 was due to 3% organic sales growth, a 5% increase from acquisitions and an unfavorable 1% effect of foreign currency translation. The increase in net sales for the first six months of 2019 was due to 4% organic sales growth and a 6% increase from acquisitions and an unfavorable 2% effect of foreign currency translation.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: The order backlog is growing, reflecting strong demand and organic sales growth. The increase from the previous period indicates positive momentum.

evidence.text: The Company achieved these results from organic sales growth in both EIG and EMG, contributions from the 2018 acquisitions [...] as well as our Operational Excellence initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations

evidence.text: As a specific example, the Company’s backlog of unfilled orders at June 30, 2019 was $1,681.2 million, an increase of $79.1 million or 4.9%, compared with $1,602.1 million at December 31, 2018.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a mixed trend in organic revenue growth, with evidence of acceleration in overall performance but a slight deceleration in the second quarter of 2019 compared to the previous year.

evidence.text: The Company posted record sales and operating income [...] from organic sales growth in both EIG and EMG [...].
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations

evidence.text: The increase in net sales for the first six months of 2019 was due to 4% organic sales growth and a 6% increase from acquisitions and an unfavorable 2% effect of foreign currency translation. The increase in net sales for the second quarter of 2019 was due to 3% organic sales growth, a 5% increase from acquisitions and an unfavorable 1% effect of foreign currency translation.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with accelerating overall demand supported by strong orders and backlog, but growth appears to be driven more by acquisitions and foreign currency effects rather than organic demand.

evidence.text: For the quarter ended June 30, 2019, the Company posted record sales and operating income, as well as strong orders, backlog, operating income margins, net income, diluted earnings per share and operating cash flow.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations

evidence.text: Orders for the second quarter of 2019 were $1,278.1 million, an increase of $47.3 million or 3.8%, compared with $1,230.8 million for the second quarter of 2018. The increase in orders for the second quarter of 2019 was due to 3% increase from acquisitions and a favorable 1% effect of foreign currency translation.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: The company's gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by operational excellence initiatives and strong demand, as evidenced by increases in segment operating margins from 23.8% to 24.4% in the second quarter and from 23.6% to 23.9% in the first six months of 2019.

evidence.text: The Company achieved these results from [...] contributions from the 2018 acquisitions [...] as well as our Operational Excellence initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations

evidence.text: Segment operating income, as a percentage of net sales, increased to 24.4% for the second quarter of 2019, compared with 23.8% for the second quarter of 2018. [...] The increase [...] resulted primarily from the increase in net sales noted above, as well as the benefits of the Company’s Operational Excellence initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

evidence.text: Segment operating income, as a percentage of net sales, increased to 23.9% for the first six months of 2019, compared with 23.6% for the first six months of 2018.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018

factors.summary: Management confidence is high, as evidenced by strong financial results and increased buyback authorization.

evidence.text: The Company posted record sales and operating income [...] as well as strong orders, backlog, operating income margins, net income, diluted earnings per share and operating cash flow.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations

evidence.text: Effective February 12, 2019, the Company’s Board of Directors approved an increase of $500 million in the authorization for the repurchase of the Company’s common stock.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2019 compared with the second quarter of 2018
